nameMeanings.name: Becker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Dutch: occupational name for a baker of bread, or brick and tiles, derived from Middle High German and Middle Dutch backen ‘to bake’. The German surname is also found in France (mainly Lorraine and Alsace).
Danish: occupational name for a baker, from bechere ‘baker’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a baker, from German Bäcker (compare 1 and Backer ) or Yiddish beker ‘baker’.

variants.name: Backer
meanings: German (Bäcker): variant of Becker . Compare Baecker .
Dutch: variant of   Bakker  . Compare   De Backer  .
Danish: probably of German origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Beckers
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ewald, Gerda, Hans, Klaus, Kurt, Wendelin, Wilhelmina.  French Jacques, Thierry.
Dutch, Flemish, and German: variant of Becker + the genitive case -s.

variants.name: Baecker
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegfried, Bodo, Gerhardt, Heinz, Horst, Kurt, Waltraud.
German (mainly Bäcker): variant of Becker . Compare Backer .

variants.name: Beck
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from northern Middle English bekke ‘stream’ (Old Norse bekkr).

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named Bec, for example Bec Hellouin in Eure, named with Old Norman French bec ‘stream’, from the same Old Norse root as in 1 above.
English: from the Middle English personal name Becke (Old English Becca or Beocca), of uncertain origin.

variants.name: De Beck
meanings: Flemish: short form of De Becker, see Becker , or a variant of Beckman .

variants.name: Beckermann
meanings: German: variant of Becker ‘baker’, formed with Middle High German man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Baker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Young camel’.  Abu-Bakr al-Ṣiddīq  (  573–634 ) was the Prophet's successor and the first rightly guided caliph (  632–4 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: John Baker Becker Sr. was born in 1598, in Norwich, Norfolk, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Hannah Beecher Langford. He died on 18 February 1673, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 75.
